method_atom: a two-mode interferometric parameter-estimation scheme in which an adiabatically, time-varying coupling drives the system's evolution so that it maps exactly onto a Ramsey-type interferometer augmented by a nonlinear (self-interaction) medium; because the nonlinear term makes the accumulated phase depend collectively on all N constituents, the measurement uncertainty of the nonlinear-coupling parameter scales super-linearly in N (as 1/N^{7/5}, beating the 1/N Heisenberg bound), while a linear path-difference parameter retains the 1/N^{1/2} shot-noise scaling, all evaluated on an SU(2) coherent (product) input state.
object_atom: a dynamically evolving two-mode Bose-Einstein condensate of N atoms subject to an adiabatic, time-varying Raman coupling between the two modes, prepared in the SU(2) coherent spin state, with the interatomic scattering strength and the inter-mode path-difference phase as the quantities being measured.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the adiabatic-coupling-to-nonlinear-Ramsey-interferometer mapping and its N-dependent estimation scaling (linear parameter at 1/N^{1/2}, nonlinear parameter beyond 1/N); the domain-specific part is the two-mode Raman-coupled Bose-Einstein condensate and its atomic scattering strength.
